description: Standard Latin uppercase block letter in the Tengri-137 print style.
Standard Latin letter printed in red (Tengri137 uses red labels on P05–P08).
Round Turkic runic glyph, family of Old Turkic / Orkhon-derived runiform script; Tengri-137 P02 shows 4 such glyphs in a 2x2 grid arrangement.
Round Turkic runic glyph, second instance.
Chinese character for tiān ("heaven") in Oracle Bone Script (甲骨文, late Shang dynasty). The shape resembles the digits "137" (Tesseract reads it as "137").
Chinese character for tiān in Seal Script (篆書), Qin dynasty.
Diamond with a dot inside — recurring element on Tengri137 slides.
Stylised face: circle with three dots (two eyes, one mouth or three-in-a-row). Tengri137 renders this as a recurring decorative glyph.
ASCII caret, used as exponent marker.
Multiplication sign (proper Unicode), used in the (2×23×…) and (3×11×…) factored integers on P17.
One of three interlocking horn-shapes in the Odin's triple-horn sigil.
One of seven linked rings forming the "7 RINGS - 666" sigil on P09.
One of nine linked rings forming the "9 RINGS - 666" sigil on P10.
A 3x3 magic square (P05–P08); row/column/diagonal sums to 666.
One of the 4x4 magic squares forming the six-square Odin triple-horn layout on P11.
Red stenographic label on P05, vowels removed; full form "revelation".
Red stenographic label on P06, vowels removed; full form "ezra".
Red stenographic label on P07, vowels removed; full form "2. chronik" (2 Chronicles).
Red stenographic label on P08; "?" substitutes for ö, full form "1. Könige" (1 Kings).
One of the 120 character cells in the 12x10 BURUMUT block on P23. Each cell is a single uppercase Latin letter.
Background watermark address string, hex 0x666AB731 = 1 718 922 033 decimal.
Single digit appearing in the prime factorisations on P17–P23.
Row of "-" used as a typographic fraction bar between prime factor numerator/denominator.
